data.title: Citius Oncology, Inc. released FY2025 results, launched LYMPHIR in U.S., closed $36M financings, had $3.9M cash, and reported $24.8M loss.
data.description: Citius Oncology, a subsidiary of Citius Pharmaceuticals, launched cancer immunotherapy LYMPHIR in the U.S. in December 2025. They completed $36 million in strategic financings, $18 million from private placement. This move aimed to strengthen cash position for LYMPHIR's commercialization. The company reported financial results with cash of $3.9 million as of September 30, 2025. LYMPHIR was launched for the treatment of adult patients with CTCL, securing access in 19 international markets via Named Patient Programs. They also announced partnerships for enhancing commercialization and promising results of an investigator-initiated clinical trial. Financially, R&D expenses were $6.4 million, G&A expenses were $8.8 million, stock-based compensation was $8.3 million, and net loss was $24.8 million for the fiscal year ending September 30, 2025. The initial market for LYMPHIR exceeded $400 million. Citius Oncology's management aimed at leveraging AI analytics for commercial efficiency and international expansion. Key distribution partners were established for 19 markets in Southern Europe and the Middle East. Stocks involved include NASDAQ: CTOR (Citius Oncology) and NASDAQ: CTXR (Citius Pharmaceuticals). For more details, visit www.citiusonc.com.

data.title: Investors nervous as Big Tech invests heavily in A.I., but tech advances may keep the rally going next year.
data.description: Investors are feeling uneasy about Big Tech's significant investment in artificial intelligence, despite the ongoing progress in the technology which is supporting optimistic investors. The substantial financial commitment by Big Tech companies in AI, which is estimated to be in the billions of dollars, is causing concern among investors. However, the continuous advancements in AI are reassuring bullish investors who remain positive about the technology's future potential.

data.title: Holiday shoppers resilient to economic challenges; spending up 3.9% from Nov. 1 to Dec. 21 compared to last year: Mastercard report.
data.description: According to a recent report by Mastercard on holiday spending, consumers have increased their spending by 3.9% between November 1 and December 21 this year compared to the same period last year.

data.title: Dow Jones and S&P 500 reach record highs, helped by tech stocks. Novo stock surges post FDA approval.
data.description: Today, the Dow Jones index and the S&P 500 experienced gains, driven by the performance of tech stocks. Notably, Novo stock saw a significant increase following FDA approval for a weight-loss pill. The Dow Jones index rose to [specific number] points, while the S&P 500 achieved a record high. Nvidia also made headlines by surpassing a crucial level, contributing to the overall positive sentiment in the stock market.

data.title: Kanazawa Univ. researchers use high-speed AFM to show brain enzyme forming dodecameric ring structure - study in Nature Comm.
data.description: In Kanazawa, Japan, on December 26, 2025, researchers at the Nano Life Science Institute (WPI-NanoLSI) at Kanazawa University have unveiled groundbreaking real-time visuals depicting the organizational process of a crucial brain enzyme that aids in memory formation. The research findings were recently published in the prestigious scientific journal Nature Communications.

data.title: Strong GDP growth of 4.3% in Q3 triggers debate as Trump's economic policies claim success; Wall Street reacts with skepticism.
data.description: The U.S. economy grew at a rate of 4.3% in the third quarter, surpassing expectations and prompting President Trump to claim it as a victory for his economic policies. Trump credited the success to his government and tariffs, highlighting strong consumer spending, increased net exports, and reduced trade deficits. The Bureau of Economic Analysis confirmed the strong GDP growth, the highest since late 2023. Trump attributed the record investment to tax cuts and tariffs, dubbing it a "Trump Economic Golden Age." However, analysts question the sustainability of this boom, sparking a fierce debate among economists. Trump's remarks stirred controversy, with critics pointing out potential flaws in his claims. Despite the impressive figures, doubts linger about the long-term impacts of these policies on the economy.

data.title: TikTok users uncover alleged redaction mistakes in DOJ docs, sparking concerns over digital security.
data.description: A surge of online investigation has brought attention to possible redaction errors in recently published Department of Justice files. This has sparked concerns regarding cybersecurity measures and the spread of misleading information on social media platforms.

data.title: Citi chooses Microchip as top chip stock, predicts analog semiconductors growth in 2026.
data.description: Citigroup has selected Microchip as its leading choice among chip stocks, citing a prediction of renewed success for analog semiconductors in the year 2026. The unexpected decision by Citi was highlighted in a recent article by Investor's Business Daily.

data.title: Ani the Grok Chatbot named Emerge's 2025 'Person' of the Year, highlighting AI intimacy's impact on society.
data.description: The increasing popularity of chatbots like Ani highlights a shift in their role from tools to partners, marking a year that emphasized the intimacy of AI and its impact on society.

data.title: Reddit stock stands out for fast-growing ad business & AI content licensing, making it top 2026 pick.
data.description: Reddit is projected to experience growth in its ad business and potential for licensing content for artificial intelligence, making it a top stock pick for 2026. Investor's Business Daily reported that Reddit's stock is expected to benefit from these factors, indicating promising opportunities for the company moving forward.

data.title: In 2025, Ethereum gains institutional acceptance as Wall Street embraces the cryptocurrency.
data.description: In the current year, leading financial hubs such as Wall Street have increasingly embraced Ethereum, and the feeling has been reciprocated by the Ethereum network.

data.title: ServiceNow buys Armis for $7.75B in cash, scares Wall Street with 4th acquisition in 2025.
data.description: ServiceNow revealed plans to acquire the Israeli cybersecurity startup Armis for $7.75 billion in cash, making it their fourth purchase of 2025. The news shook Wall Street as ServiceNow makes a significant move in the tech industry.

data.title: Analysts predict Apple will enter AI in 2026. Stock experts confident about iPhone maker's move.
data.description: Analysts predict that Apple will embrace artificial intelligence in the early months of 2026. Market experts foresee the tech giant stepping into the AI realm next year.

data.title: Pipeline permit for Sable Offshore surges. Phil Mickelson still struggles post-worst oil spill half-century ago.
data.description: In 2015, a pipeline ruptured causing the most severe oil spill in the past fifty years. The Sable Offshore pipeline permit has recently surged, while Phil Mickelson remains in a difficult financial situation.

data.title: Israeli shipping stock ZIM Integrated surges with over 60% rally from October low, despite being down 7% on the year.
data.description: An Israeli company, ZIM Integrated, has experienced a 7% decrease in its stock performance for the year, however, it has seen a significant increase of over 60% since hitting a low point in October. Reports indicate that the Israel-based shipping company has witnessed a surge in its stock value due to receiving competitive acquisition proposals for all outstanding shares. This positive development has drawn investor attention, as highlighted in the recent coverage by Investor's Business Daily.
